letters,
A-E
.

obj.content: Which [b] TWO [/b] things do the students agree they need to include in their review of Romeo and Juliet?
obj.html_content: Which TWO things do the students agree they need to include in their review of Romeo and Juliet?

obj.content: analysis of the text
obj.html_content: analysis of the text

obj.content: a summary of the plot
obj.html_content: a summary of the plot

obj.content: a description of the theatre
obj.html_content: a description of the theatre

obj.content: a personal reaction
obj.html_content: a personal reaction

obj.content: a reference to particular scenes
obj.html_content: a reference to particular scenes

sentence.content: Ed: Did you make notes while you were watching the performances of Romeo and Juliet, Gemma?
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:杰玛，你看《罗密欧与朱丽叶》的时候做笔记了吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes, I did. I found it quite hard though. I kept getting too involved in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:做了。但我发现这很难。我总是太投入到戏里。

sentence.content: Ed: Me too. I ended up not taking notes. I wrote down my impressions when I got home. Do you mind if I check a few things with you? In case I’ve missed anything. And I’ve also got some questions about our assignment.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我也是。最后我没有做笔记。我回家后就把印象写了下来。你介意我和你对一下吗?以防我错过什么。我对我们的作业也有些疑问。

sentence.content: Gemma: No, it’s good to talk things through. I may have missed things too.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:不，把问题讨论清楚很好。我可能也错过了一些东西。

sentence.content: Ed: OK great. So first of all, I’m not sure how much information we should include in our reviews.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:好的太棒了。所以首先，我不确定我们应该在剧评中包含多少信息。

sentence.content: Gemma: Right. Well, I don’t think we need to describe what happens. Especially as Romeo and Juliet is one of Shakespeare’s most well-known plays.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是吧。我觉得我们不需要描述发生了什么。尤其是《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是莎士比亚最著名的戏剧之一。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah, everyone knows the story. In an essay we’d focus on the poetry and Shakespeare’s use of imagery etc., but that isn’t really relevant in a review. We’re supposed to focus on how effective this particular production is.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的，每个人都知道这个故事。如果是写论文的话，我们会关注（戏剧中的）诗歌，以及莎士比亚对意象的巧妙运用等等，但这些内容对于剧评来说并不切题。我们应该关注的是这个剧院上映的这部剧是否达到了预期的效果。

sentence.content: Gemma: Mmm. We should say what made it a success or a failure.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:嗯。我们应该说出是什么导致了它的成功或失败。

sentence.content: Ed: And part of that means talking about the emotional impact the performance had on us. I think that’s important.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:还有一部分是谈论演出给我们带来的情感影响。我认为这很重要。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. And we should definitely mention how well the director handled important bits of the play – like when Romeo climbs onto Juliet’s balcony.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。我们应该提到导演对剧中重要部分的处理有多好，比如当罗密欧爬上朱丽叶的阳台时。

sentence.content: Ed: And the fight between Mercutio and Tybalt.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:还有茂丘西奥和提伯尔特之间的争斗。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. It would also be interesting to mention the theatre space and how the director used it but I don’t think we’ll have space in 800 words.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。（在剧评中）写写剧场空间以及导演如何利用空间也很有意思，但800字的篇幅有限，这部分恐怕无法加进去。

sentence.content: Ed: No. OK. That all sounds quite straightforward.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:没有。好的。这一切听起来都很简单。

sentence.content: Ed: So what about The Emporium Theatre’s production of the play?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:那Emporium剧院的演出怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I thought some things worked really well but there were some problems too.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我认为有些东西效果很好，不过也有一些问题。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah. What about the set, for example?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的。比如，布景怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I think it was visually really stunning. I’d say that was probably the most memorable thing about this production.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛: 我认为这部剧的视觉效果真的很棒。要我说，这恐怕是整个作品中最令人难忘的地方。

sentence.content: Ed: You’re right. The set design was really amazing, but actually I have seen similar ideas used in other productions.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你是对的。布景设计真的很棒，但实际上我在其他作品中看到过类似的想法。

sentence.content: Gemma: What about the lighting? Some of the scenes were so dimly lit it was quite hard to see.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:灯光怎么样?有些场景光线很暗，很难看清。

sentence.content: Ed: I didn’t dislike it. It helped to change the mood of the quieter scenes.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我并不反感。它有助于改变安静场景的气氛。

sentence.content: Gemma: That’s a good point.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:有道理。

sentence.content: Ed: What did you think of the costumes?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你觉得戏服怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I was a bit surprised by the contemporary dress, I must say.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我必须说，我对现代风格的服装有点惊讶。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah – I think it worked well, but I had assumed it would be more conventional.
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:是的，我认为它很有效，但我以为它会更传统。

sentence.content: Gemma: Me too. I liked the music at the beginning and I thought the musicians were brilliant, but I thought they were wasted because the music didn’t have much impact in Acts 2 and 3.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我也是。我喜欢一开始的音乐，我认为音乐家们很出色，但我认为他们后面被埋没了，因为音乐在第二章和第三章没有太大的影响。

sentence.content: Ed: Yes – that was a shame.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的，真遗憾。

sentence.content: Gemma: One problem with this production was that the actors didn’t deliver the lines that well. They were speaking too fast.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:这部剧的一个问题是演员们没有很好地表达台词。他们说得太快了。

sentence.content: Ed: It was a problem I agree, but I thought it was because they weren’t speaking loudly enough – especially at key points in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:我同意这是一个问题，但我认为这是因为他们说话不够大声——尤其是在剧中的关键时刻。

sentence.content: Gemma: I actually didn’t have a problem with that.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:实际我觉得还好。

sentence.content: Ed: It’s been an interesting experience watching different versions of Romeo and Juliet, hasn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:看不同版本的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是很有趣的经历，不是吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: Definitely. It’s made me realise how relevant the play still is.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:当然。这让我意识到这出戏仍然很重要。

sentence.content: Ed: Right. I mean a lot’s changed since Shakespeare’s time, but in many ways nothing’s changed. There are always disagreements and tension between teenagers and their parents.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:对的。我的意思是，自莎士比亚时代以来，很多事情都发生了变化，但在很多方面都没有改变。青少年和父母之间总是有分歧和紧张。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes, that’s something all young people can relate to – more than the violence and the extreme emotions in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 是的，这是所有年轻人都能感受到的——比剧中的暴力和极端情绪更能让他们感同身受。

sentence.content: Ed: How did you find watching it in translation?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你觉得看翻译版怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: Really interesting. I expected to find it more challenging, but I could follow the story pretty well.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:真的很有趣。我原以为会更有挑战性，但我能很好地理解这个故事。

sentence.content: Ed: I stopped worrying about not being able to understand all the words and focused on the actors’ expressions. The ending was pretty powerful.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我不再担心不能理解所有的单词，而是专注于演员的表情。结局相当震撼。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. That somehow intensified the emotion for me.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。这在某种程度上加剧了我的情绪。

sentence.content: Ed: Did you know Shakespeare’s been translated into more languages than any other writer?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你知道莎士比亚被翻译成的语言比其他任何作家都多吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: What’s the reason for his international appeal, do you think?
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:你认为是什么原因让他蜚声国际?

sentence.content: Ed: I was reading that it’s because his plays are about basic themes that people everywhere are familiar with.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我读到这是因为他的戏剧都是关于世界各地的人都熟悉的基本主题。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yeah, and they can also be understood on different levels. The characters have such depth.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:对，它们也可以在不同的层面上被理解。这些人物都很有深度。

sentence.content: Ed: Right – which allows directors to experiment and find new angles.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:没错，这让导演们可以尝试和寻找新的角度。

sentence.content: Gemma: That’s really important because …
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:这很重要，因为……

sentence.content: Gemma: That’s really important because …
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:这很重要，因为……

obj.html_content: The students think the story of Romeo and Juliet is still relevant for young people today because

sentence.content: Ed: Did you make notes while you were watching the performances of Romeo and Juliet, Gemma?
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:杰玛，你看《罗密欧与朱丽叶》的时候做笔记了吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes, I did. I found it quite hard though. I kept getting too involved in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:做了。但我发现这很难。我总是太投入到戏里。

sentence.content: Ed: Me too. I ended up not taking notes. I wrote down my impressions when I got home. Do you mind if I check a few things with you? In case I’ve missed anything. And I’ve also got some questions about our assignment.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我也是。最后我没有做笔记。我回家后就把印象写了下来。你介意我和你对一下吗?以防我错过什么。我对我们的作业也有些疑问。

sentence.content: Gemma: No, it’s good to talk things through. I may have missed things too.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:不，把问题讨论清楚很好。我可能也错过了一些东西。

sentence.content: Ed: OK great. So first of all, I’m not sure how much information we should include in our reviews.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:好的太棒了。所以首先，我不确定我们应该在剧评中包含多少信息。

sentence.content: Gemma: Right. Well, I don’t think we need to describe what happens. Especially as Romeo and Juliet is one of Shakespeare’s most well-known plays.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是吧。我觉得我们不需要描述发生了什么。尤其是《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是莎士比亚最著名的戏剧之一。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah, everyone knows the story. In an essay we’d focus on the poetry and Shakespeare’s use of imagery etc., but that isn’t really relevant in a review. We’re supposed to focus on how effective this particular production is.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的，每个人都知道这个故事。如果是写论文的话，我们会关注（戏剧中的）诗歌，以及莎士比亚对意象的巧妙运用等等，但这些内容对于剧评来说并不切题。我们应该关注的是这个剧院上映的这部剧是否达到了预期的效果。

sentence.content: Gemma: Mmm. We should say what made it a success or a failure.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:嗯。我们应该说出是什么导致了它的成功或失败。

sentence.content: Ed: And part of that means talking about the emotional impact the performance had on us. I think that’s important.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:还有一部分是谈论演出给我们带来的情感影响。我认为这很重要。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. And we should definitely mention how well the director handled important bits of the play – like when Romeo climbs onto Juliet’s balcony.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。我们应该提到导演对剧中重要部分的处理有多好，比如当罗密欧爬上朱丽叶的阳台时。

sentence.content: Ed: And the fight between Mercutio and Tybalt.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:还有茂丘西奥和提伯尔特之间的争斗。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. It would also be interesting to mention the theatre space and how the director used it but I don’t think we’ll have space in 800 words.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。（在剧评中）写写剧场空间以及导演如何利用空间也很有意思，但800字的篇幅有限，这部分恐怕无法加进去。

sentence.content: Ed: No. OK. That all sounds quite straightforward.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:没有。好的。这一切听起来都很简单。

sentence.content: Ed: So what about The Emporium Theatre’s production of the play?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:那Emporium剧院的演出怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I thought some things worked really well but there were some problems too.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我认为有些东西效果很好，不过也有一些问题。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah. What about the set, for example?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的。比如，布景怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I think it was visually really stunning. I’d say that was probably the most memorable thing about this production.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛: 我认为这部剧的视觉效果真的很棒。要我说，这恐怕是整个作品中最令人难忘的地方。

sentence.content: Ed: You’re right. The set design was really amazing, but actually I have seen similar ideas used in other productions.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你是对的。布景设计真的很棒，但实际上我在其他作品中看到过类似的想法。

sentence.content: Gemma: What about the lighting? Some of the scenes were so dimly lit it was quite hard to see.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:灯光怎么样?有些场景光线很暗，很难看清。

sentence.content: Ed: I didn’t dislike it. It helped to change the mood of the quieter scenes.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我并不反感。它有助于改变安静场景的气氛。

sentence.content: Gemma: That’s a good point.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:有道理。

sentence.content: Ed: What did you think of the costumes?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你觉得戏服怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: I was a bit surprised by the contemporary dress, I must say.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我必须说，我对现代风格的服装有点惊讶。

sentence.content: Ed: Yeah – I think it worked well, but I had assumed it would be more conventional.
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:是的，我认为它很有效，但我以为它会更传统。

sentence.content: Gemma: Me too. I liked the music at the beginning and I thought the musicians were brilliant, but I thought they were wasted because the music didn’t have much impact in Acts 2 and 3.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:我也是。我喜欢一开始的音乐，我认为音乐家们很出色，但我认为他们后面被埋没了，因为音乐在第二章和第三章没有太大的影响。

sentence.content: Ed: Yes – that was a shame.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:是的，真遗憾。

sentence.content: Gemma: One problem with this production was that the actors didn’t deliver the lines that well. They were speaking too fast.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:这部剧的一个问题是演员们没有很好地表达台词。他们说得太快了。

sentence.content: Ed: It was a problem I agree, but I thought it was because they weren’t speaking loudly enough – especially at key points in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 埃德:我同意这是一个问题，但我认为这是因为他们说话不够大声——尤其是在剧中的关键时刻。

sentence.content: Gemma: I actually didn’t have a problem with that.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:实际我觉得还好。

sentence.content: Ed: It’s been an interesting experience watching different versions of Romeo and Juliet, hasn’t it?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:看不同版本的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》是很有趣的经历，不是吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: Definitely. It’s made me realise how relevant the play still is.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:当然。这让我意识到这出戏仍然很重要。

sentence.content: Ed: Right. I mean a lot’s changed since Shakespeare’s time, but in many ways nothing’s changed. There are always disagreements and tension between teenagers and their parents.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:对的。我的意思是，自莎士比亚时代以来，很多事情都发生了变化，但在很多方面都没有改变。青少年和父母之间总是有分歧和紧张。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes, that’s something all young people can relate to – more than the violence and the extreme emotions in the play.
sentence.content_zh: 是的，这是所有年轻人都能感受到的——比剧中的暴力和极端情绪更能让他们感同身受。

sentence.content: Ed: How did you find watching it in translation?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你觉得看翻译版怎么样?

sentence.content: Gemma: Really interesting. I expected to find it more challenging, but I could follow the story pretty well.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:真的很有趣。我原以为会更有挑战性，但我能很好地理解这个故事。

sentence.content: Ed: I stopped worrying about not being able to understand all the words and focused on the actors’ expressions. The ending was pretty powerful.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我不再担心不能理解所有的单词，而是专注于演员的表情。结局相当震撼。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yes. That somehow intensified the emotion for me.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:是的。这在某种程度上加剧了我的情绪。

sentence.content: Ed: Did you know Shakespeare’s been translated into more languages than any other writer?
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:你知道莎士比亚被翻译成的语言比其他任何作家都多吗?

sentence.content: Gemma: What’s the reason for his international appeal, do you think?
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:你认为是什么原因让他蜚声国际?

sentence.content: Ed: I was reading that it’s because his plays are about basic themes that people everywhere are familiar with.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:我读到这是因为他的戏剧都是关于世界各地的人都熟悉的基本主题。

sentence.content: Gemma: Yeah, and they can also be understood on different levels. The characters have such depth.
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:对，它们也可以在不同的层面上被理解。这些人物都很有深度。

sentence.content: Ed: Right – which allows directors to experiment and find new angles.
sentence.content_zh: 艾德:没错，这让导演们可以尝试和寻找新的角度。

sentence.content: Gemma: That’s really important because …
sentence.content_zh: 杰玛:这很重要，因为……

officialExplain.explain: 本题需要选择两名学生写剧评时需要包含的内容。女生Gemma首先否定了describe what happens，对应 B选项a summary of the plot，因此可排除B选项。男生肯定了女生的说法后，接着说到如果写一篇essay需要聚焦poetry and imagery，即对应A选项的analysis of the text，随即在转折词but后否定：这对于review而言不太切题，因此排除A选项。后文男生又提到emotional impact the performance had on us，并且认为这一点important，对应D选项personal reaction。女生通过Yes表达了肯定，可确定D选项正确。女生接着提出是否应该写写how well the director handled important bits of the play，即E选项的particular scenes，如果这里原文理解有困难，可以通过女生给的例子when Romeo climbs onto Juliet’s balcony辅助理解，之后听到男生给予肯定回答，因此可确认E选项。最后女生提到theatre space，对应C选项，但but后说篇幅不够，否定了该选项。
D选项中的strength通过原文一系列褒义词体现，即女生所说的stunning、most memorable，以及男生表达赞同后所说的amazing。随后男生提到I have seen similar ideas used in other productions，即否定了set的原创性，据此可排除B选项。
题干词lighting出现后，女生首先说灯光太暗看不清，但男生没有表达赞同。同时，男生认为较暗的灯光是为了契合安静的场景，C选项中的atmosphere替换为mood。女生通过That’s a good point表达赞同，可确定C选项。
女生首先说a bit surprised by the contemporary dress，即说明和预期不符，为A选项中expect…more traditional的反向表达，即“本以为服饰会更传统”。之后男生也表达了同样观点，选项中的expect和traditional分别替换为assume和conventional。
E选项中的disappointed通过女生所说的wasted及didn’t have much impact体现。随后男生所说的a shame指“令人遗憾的事”，也表达了disappointed的意思。

女生认为演员说台词问题在于too fast，而男生首先肯定了台词有问题，之后说他认为台词的问题在于weren’t speaking loudly enough。女生在回答中表示自己没觉得有这方面的问题，即两人关于到底台词哪方面出问题有分歧，对应F选项中的why this was an issue。此题易误选G选项，但原文描述的是问题所在，而非how this could be improved，G选项不准确。

通过女生所说的how relevant the play still is可初步定位。随后男生说到disagreements and tension between teenagers and their parents, 其中disagreements和tension对应选项B中的problems，teenagers and their parents对应选项中的families。随后女生表示赞同，说到这一点all young people can relate to，可确定B选项。A选项中虽然有conflict一词，但how easily…can start在原文中没有体现。C选项中的relationships虽在原文也有涉及，但原文强调的是莎士比亚戏剧展现了青少年和父母的矛盾，令当今的年轻人感同身受，并非教给年轻人如何处理关系。

题干中的another language在原文中替换为translation。女生首先说到expected to find it challenging ，即预想很难，但转折词but后否定了该说法，因此可排除B选项。男生首先说stop worrying about not being able to understand…，可否定A选项。随后男生提到戏剧结尾powerful，即很震撼，女生也表示赞同，说intensified the emotion，即结尾部分让整个戏剧带给人的感受更强烈，对应C选项moving。本题需要考生熟悉三个选项的单词含义。

题干中的international appeal原词重现。男生首先说到莎士比亚的戏剧题材亲民，但没有对应的选项。女生补充说they can also be understood on different levels，understood对应C选项的interpreted，different levels对应many ways；男生下一句回应中的experiment and find new angles也可以对应C选项，可辅助验证答案。A选项未提及。B选项的characters虽然在女生讲话中原词重现，但原文为the characters have such depth，即角色有深度，B选项的recognisable并未体现。
